entries.detail:
1→/// TUI application state and event types.
     2→///
     3→/// Defines the core data model for the kanban TUI: columns, card views,
     4→/// navigation state, and the event enum that unifies keyboard input,
     5→/// filesystem changes, and timer ticks into a single async stream.
     6→use std::collections::VecDeque;
     7→use std::fs;
     8→use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
     9→
    10→use chrono::Utc;
    11→use crossterm::event::KeyEvent;
    12→use ratatui::widgets::ListState;
    13→
    14→use crate::providers::read_providers;
    15→use crate::render::CardView;
    16→use crate::ui::widgets::action::actions_for_state;
    17→use crate::ui::widgets::header::{ProviderMeter, ProviderStatus, TICKER_CAPACITY};
    18→
    19→// ── Events ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    20→
    21→/// Unified event type for the TUI event loop.
    22→///
    23→/// Background tasks (fs watcher, log reader, timer) send these through
    24→/// `tokio::sync::mpsc::unbounded_channel`. The UI loop processes them
    25→/// sequentially — never blocking the render thread.
    26→#[derive(Debug)]
    27→pub enum AppEvent {
    28→    /// Keyboard input from crossterm.
    29→    Key(KeyEvent),
    30→    /// Terminal resize (cols, rows).
    31→    Resize(u16, u16),
    32→    /// 250ms heartbeat for clock, sparkline, and animation updates.
    33→    Tick,
    34→    /// Filesystem watcher detected card changes — full refresh payload.
    35→    Cards(Vec<KanbanColumn>),
    36→    /// New complete line from the selected card's log tail.
    37→    LogLine(String),
    38→}
    39→
    40→// ── Mode ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    41→
    42→/// Current interaction mode. Determines which keybindings are active
    43→/// and which overlay (if any) is drawn on top of the kanban body.
    44→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    45→pub enum Mode {
    46→    /// Default: column/card navigation, action keys active.
    47→    Normal,
    48→    /// `/` filter active — keystrokes go to nucleo query input.
    49→    Filter,
    50→    /// Enter key opened the action popup overlay.
    51→    ActionPopup,
    52→    /// `d` opened the card detail overlay.
    53→    Detail,
    54→    /// Log tail overlay — full-height scrollable log view.
    55→    LogTail,
    56→    /// `n` inline card creation prompt.
    57→    NewCard,
    58→    /// `!` subshell — TUI suspended, shell active in card worktree.
    59→    Subshell,
    60→}
    61→
    62→// ── KanbanColumn ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    63→
    64→/// Column state names in display order.
    65→pub const COLUMN_STATES: &[&str] = &["pending", "running", "done", "failed", "merged"];
    66→
    67→/// One vertical column in the kanban board.
    68→///
    69→/// Each column corresponds to a card state directory. The `list_state`
    70→/// tracks the currently selected row within the column for ratatui's
    71→/// `StatefulWidget` rendering.
    72→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    73→pub struct KanbanColumn {
    74→    /// State name (e.g. "pending", "running").
    75→    pub state: String,
    76→    /// Cards currently in this state.
    77→    pub cards: Vec<CardView>,
    78→    /// Ratatui selection state for the list widget.
    79→    pub list_state: ListState,
    80→    /// WIP limit for this column (from config `max_concurrent` for "running").
    81→    pub wip_limit: Option<usize>,
    82→    /// Auto-collapsed when card count is 0.
    83→    pub collapsed: bool,
    84→}
    85→
    86→impl KanbanColumn {
    87→    /// Create a new column for the given state with cards.
    88→    pub(crate) fn new(state: &str, cards: Vec<CardView>, wip_limit: Option<usize>) -> Self {
    89→        let collapsed = cards.is_empty();
    90→        let mut list_state = ListState::default();
    91→        if !cards.is_empty() {
    92→            list_state.select(Some(0));
    93→        }
    94→        Self {
    95→            state: state.to_string(),
    96→            cards,
    97→            list_state,
    98→            wip_limit,
    99→            collapsed,
   100→        }
   101→    }
   102→
   103→    /// Returns true when the column is at or over its WIP limit.
   104→    pub fn is_at_wip_limit(&self) -> bool {
   105→        match self.wip_limit {
   106→            Some(limit) => self.cards.len() >= limit,
   107→            None => false,
   108→        }
   109→    }
   110→
   111→    /// WIP saturation fraction (0.0–1.0). Returns 0.0 when no limit is set.
   112→    pub fn wip_saturation(&self) -> f32 {
   113→        match self.wip_limit {
   114→            Some(limit) if limit > 0 => (self.cards.len() as f32 / limit as f32).min(1.0),
   115→            _ => 0.0,
   116→        }
   117→    }
   118→}
   119→
   120→// ── App ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   121→
   122→/// Top-level TUI application state.
   123→///
   124→/// Holds all columns, focus indices, mode, log buffer, and throughput
   125→/// sparkline data. The render layer reads this immutably; event handlers
   126→/// mutate it.
   127→pub struct App {
   128→    /// Kanban columns in display order (pending → running → done → failed → merged).
   129→    pub columns: Vec<KanbanColumn>,
   130→    /// Index into `columns` for the currently focused column.
   131→    pub col_focus: usize,
   132→    /// Active nucleo filter query (None = no filter).
   133→    pub filter: Option<String>,
   134→    /// Current interaction mode.
   135→    pub mode: Mode,
   136→    /// Circular buffer of recent log lines (last 200) from the selected card.
   137→    pub log_buf: VecDeque<String>,
   138→    /// Throughput sparkline samples (last 8 values, cards/hr).
   139→    pub throughput: VecDeque<u8>,
   140→    /// Root path of the .cards directory.
   141→    pub cards_root: PathBuf,
   142→    /// Provider status meters for the header bar.
   143→    pub provider_meters: Vec<ProviderMeter>,
   144→    /// Recent card completions for the scrolling event ticker.
   145→    pub recent_completions: VecDeque<String>,
   146→    /// Monotonic tick counter for ticker scroll animation.
   147→    pub tick_count: u64,
   148→    /// Previous "done" card count for throughput delta tracking.
   149→    pub prev_done_count: usize,
   150→    /// Scroll offset for the Detail overlay (lines scrolled from top).
   151→    pub detail_scroll: usize,
   152→    /// Ratatui selection state for the ActionPopup list widget.
   153→    pub action_list_state: ListState,
   154→}
   155→
   156→/// Maximum number of log lines retained in the circular buffer.
   157→const LOG_BUF_CAPACITY: usize = 200;
   158→
   159→/// Number of throughput sparkline samples.
   160→const THROUGHPUT_SAMPLES: usize = 8;
   161→
   162→impl App {
   163→    /// Create a new App by scanning the filesystem for cards.
   164→    ///
   165→    /// Reads cards from the `.cards` directory using the same pattern as
   166→    /// `list.rs::collect_card_views`. Reads `max_concurrent` from the
   167→    /// bop-core config for the "running" column's WIP limit.
   168→    pub fn new(cards_root: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<Self> {
   169→        let columns = build_columns(cards_root);
   170→
   171→        // Focus the first non-collapsed column, or 0 if all are collapsed.
   172→        let col_focus = columns
   173→            .iter()
   174→            .position(|c| !c.collapsed)
   175→            .unwrap_or(0);
   176→
   177→        let done_count = columns
   178→            .iter()
   179→            .find(|c| c.state == "done")
   180→            .map(|c| c.cards.len())
   181→            .unwrap_or(0);
   182→
   183→        let mut app = App {
   184→            columns,
   185→            col_focus,
   186→            filter: None,
   187→            mode: Mode::Normal,
   188→            log_buf: VecDeque::with_capacity(LOG_BUF_CAPACITY),
   189→            throughput: VecDeque::from(vec![0u8; THROUGHPUT_SAMPLES]),
   190→            cards_root: cards_root.to_path_buf(),
   191→            provider_meters: Vec::new(),
   192→            recent_completions: VecDeque::with_capacity(TICKER_CAPACITY),
   193→            tick_count: 0,
   194→            prev_done_count: done_count,
   195→            detail_scroll: 0,
   196→            action_list_state: ListState::default(),
   197→        };
   198→
   199→        // Seed provider meters from providers.json.
   200→        app.refresh_provider_meters();
   201→
   202→        Ok(app)
   203→    }
   204→
   205→    /// Return the currently focused column, if any.
   206→    pub fn focused_column(&self) -> Option<&KanbanColumn> {
   207→        self.columns.get(self.col_focus)
   208→    }
   209→
   210→    /// Return a mutable reference to the currently focused column.
   211→    pub fn focused_column_mut(&mut self) -> Option<&mut KanbanColumn> {
   212→        self.columns.get_mut(self.col_focus)
   213→    }
   214→
   215→    /// Return the currently selected card in the focused column.
   216→    pub fn selected_card(&self) -> Option<&CardView> {
   217→        let col = self.focused_column()?;
   218→        let idx = col.list_state.selected()?;
   219→        col.cards.get(idx)
   220→    }
   221→
   222→    /// Total card count across all columns.
   223→    pub fn total_cards(&self) -> usize {
   224→        self.columns.iter().map(|c| c.cards.len()).sum()
   225→    }
   226→
   227→    /// Open the action popup for the currently selected card.
   228→    ///
   229→    /// Initialises `action_list_state` with the first item selected
   230→    /// (if actions are available) and switches to `Mode::ActionPopup`.
   231→    /// No-op if no card is selected.
   232→    pub fn open_action_popup(&mut self) {
   233→        if let Some(card) = self.selected_card() {
   234→            let actions = actions_for_state(&card.state);
   235→            let mut state = ListState::default();
   236→            if !actions.is_empty() {
   237→                state.select(Some(0));
   238→            }
   239→            self.action_list_state = state;

1→/// Detail overlay widget — card information panel on right 50% of body.
     2→///
     3→/// Renders on top of the kanban board using ratatui `Clear` + bordered
     4→/// `Block`. Shows: card id, provider, elapsed time, progress bar,
     5→/// phase/subtask tree from AC plan (`implementation_plan.json` in the
     6→/// card directory, if present). Scrollable with j/k.
     7→///
     8→/// Footer keybinds (rendered by the footer widget, not here):
     9→/// `[F3]logs [p]pause [r]retry [Esc]close`
    10→use std::fs;
    11→use std::path::Path;
    12→
    13→use ratatui::layout::Rect;
    14→use ratatui::style::{Color, Modifier, Style};
    15→use ratatui::text::{Line, Span};
    16→use ratatui::widgets::{Block, Clear, Paragraph, Wrap};
    17→use ratatui::Frame;
    18→
    19→use crate::render::CardView;
    20→
    21→// ── AC plan types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    22→
    23→/// A subtask entry from an AC `implementation_plan.json`.
    24→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    25→struct PlanSubtask {
    26→    id: String,
    27→    description: String,
    28→    status: String,
    29→}
    30→
    31→/// A phase entry from an AC `implementation_plan.json`.
    32→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    33→struct PlanPhase {
    34→    name: String,
    35→    subtasks: Vec<PlanSubtask>,
    36→}
    37→
    38→/// Parsed AC plan data (phases + subtasks).
    39→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    40→struct AcPlan {
    41→    phases: Vec<PlanPhase>,
    42→}
    43→
    44→// ── Plan parser ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    45→
    46→/// Read and parse `implementation_plan.json` from a card directory.
    47→///
    48→/// Returns `None` if the file doesn't exist or can't be parsed.
    49→/// Tolerant of missing fields — extracts what it can.
    50→fn read_ac_plan(card_dir: &Path) -> Option<AcPlan> {
    51→    let plan_path = card_dir.join("implementation_plan.json");
    52→    let content = fs::read_to_string(&plan_path).ok()?;
    53→    let root: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&content).ok()?;
    54→
    55→    let phases_arr = root.get("phases")?.as_array()?;
    56→    let mut phases = Vec::new();
    57→
    58→    for phase_val in phases_arr {
    59→        let name = phase_val
    60→            .get("name")
    61→            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
    62→            .unwrap_or("unnamed")
    63→            .to_string();
    64→
    65→        let mut subtasks = Vec::new();
    66→        if let Some(subs) = phase_val.get("subtasks").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
    67→            for sub in subs {
    68→                let id = sub
    69→                    .get("id")
    70→                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
    71→                    .unwrap_or("")
    72→                    .to_string();
    73→                let description = sub
    74→                    .get("description")
    75→                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
    76→                    .unwrap_or("")
    77→                    .to_string();
    78→                let status = sub
    79→                    .get("status")
    80→                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
    81→                    .unwrap_or("pending")
    82→                    .to_string();
    83→
    84→                subtasks.push(PlanSubtask {
    85→                    id,
    86→                    description,
    87→                    status,
    88→                });
    89→            }
    90→        }
    91→
    92→        phases.push(PlanPhase { name, subtasks });
    93→    }
    94→
    95→    Some(AcPlan { phases })
    96→}
    97→
    98→// ── Rendering helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    99→
   100→/// Status glyph for a subtask status string.
   101→fn status_glyph(status: &str) -> &'static str {
   102→    match status {
   103→        "completed" => "✓",
   104→        "in_progress" => "▶",
   105→        "failed" => "✗",
   106→        _ => "·", // pending or unknown
   107→    }
   108→}
   109→
   110→/// Status color for a subtask status string.
   111→fn status_color(status: &str) -> Color {
   112→    match status {
   113→        "completed" => Color::Rgb(0x1E, 0x8A, 0x45),  // green
   114→        "in_progress" => Color::Rgb(0xB8, 0x69, 0x0F), // amber
   115→        "failed" => Color::Rgb(0xC4, 0x30, 0x30),      // red
   116→        _ => Color::DarkGray,                           // pending
   117→    }
   118→}
   119→
   120→/// Format seconds into human-readable "XmYYs" or "Xs".
   121→fn format_duration(secs: u64) -> String {
   122→    if secs >= 3600 {
   123→        format!("{}h{:02}m", secs / 3600, (secs % 3600) / 60)
   124→    } else if secs >= 60 {
   125→        format!("{}m{:02}s", secs / 60, secs % 60)
   126→    } else {
   127→        format!("{}s", secs)
   128→    }
   129→}
   130→
   131→/// Build the progress bar line.
   132→///
   133→/// Format: `████████░░░░░░░░░░░░ 42%`
   134→fn build_progress_bar(pct: u8, width: usize) -> Line<'static> {
   135→    let bar_width = width.saturating_sub(6).min(30); // reserve space for " NNN%"
   136→    let filled = (pct as usize * bar_width) / 100;
   137→    let empty = bar_width.saturating_sub(filled);
   138→
   139→    let bar = format!("{}{}", "█".repeat(filled), "░".repeat(empty));
   140→
   141→    let color = if pct >= 75 {
   142→        Color::Rgb(0x1E, 0x8A, 0x45) // green
   143→    } else if pct >= 40 {
   144→        Color::Rgb(0xB8, 0x69, 0x0F) // amber
   145→    } else {
   146→        Color::Rgb(0x3A, 0x5A, 0x8A) // blue
   147→    };
   148→
   149→    Line::from(vec![
   150→        Span::styled(bar, Style::default().fg(color)),
   151→        Span::styled(
   152→            format!(" {:>3}%", pct),
   153→            Style::default().fg(Color::White),
   154→        ),
   155→    ])
   156→}
   157→
   158→/// Build all content lines for the detail overlay.
   159→///
   160→/// Returns a `Vec<Line>` that can be scrolled via `detail_scroll`.
   161→fn build_detail_lines(card: &CardView, card_dir: Option<&Path>) -> Vec<Line<'static>> {
   162→    let mut lines: Vec<Line<'static>> = Vec::new();
   163→
   164→    // ── Card ID ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   165→    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   166→        Span::styled(
   167→            "Card: ",
   168→            Style::default()
   169→                .fg(Color::DarkGray)
   170→                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
   171→        ),
   172→        Span::styled(card.id.clone(), Style::default().fg(Color::White)),
   173→    ]));
   174→
   175→    // ── State ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   176→    let state_color = match card.state.as_str() {
   177→        "pending" => Color::Rgb(0x3A, 0x5A, 0x8A),
   178→        "running" => Color::Rgb(0xB8, 0x69, 0x0F),
   179→        "done" => Color::Rgb(0x1E, 0x8A, 0x45),
   180→        "failed" => Color::Rgb(0xC4, 0x30, 0x30),
   181→        "merged" => Color::Rgb(0x6B, 0x3D, 0xB8),
   182→        _ => Color::Gray,
   183→    };
   184→    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   185→        Span::styled(
   186→            "State: ",
   187→            Style::default()
   188→                .fg(Color::DarkGray)
   189→                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
   190→        ),
   191→        Span::styled(
   192→            card.state.clone(),
   193→            Style::default().fg(state_color),
   194→        ),
   195→    ]));
   196→
   197→    // ── Stage ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   198→    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   199→        Span::styled(
   200→            "Stage: ",
   201→            Style::default()
   202→                .fg(Color::DarkGray)
   203→                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
   204→        ),
   205→        Span::styled(card.stage.clone(), Style::default().fg(Color::White)),
   206→    ]));
   207→
   208→    // ── Provider ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   209→    if let Some(ref provider) = card.provider {
   210→        lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   211→            Span::styled(
   212→                "Provider: ",
   213→                Style::default()
   214→                    .fg(Color::DarkGray)
   215→                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
   216→            ),
   217→            Span::styled(provider.clone(), Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
   218→        ]));
   219→    }
   220→
   221→    // ── Elapsed ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   222→    if let Some(elapsed) = card.elapsed_s {
   223→        lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   224→            Span::styled(
   225→                "Elapsed: ",
   226→                Style::default()
   227→                    .fg(Color::DarkGray)
   228→                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
   229→            ),
   230→            Span::styled(
   231→                format_duration(elapsed),
   232→                Style::default().fg(Color::White),
   233→            ),
   234→        ]));
   235→    }
   236→
   237→    // ── Priority ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   238→    if let Some(pri) = card.priority {
   239→        let (label, color) = match pri {
   240→            1 => ("Urgent", Color::Red),
   241→            2 => ("High", Color::Yellow),
   242→            3 => ("Normal", Color::White),
   243→            4 => ("Low", Color::DarkGray),
   244→            _ => ("Unknown", Color::Gray),
   245→        };
   246→        lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   247→            Span::styled(
   248→                "Priority: ",
   249→                Style::default()
   250→                    .fg(Color::DarkGray)
   251→                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
   252→            ),
   253→            Span::styled(label, Style::default().fg(color)),
   254→        ]));
   255→    }
   256→

   258→    if let Some(ref reason) = card.failure_reason {
   259→        lines.push(Line::from(vec![

   261→                "Failure: ",

entries.content: Now let me also look at the action popup rendering and the footer to understand overlay patterns better, plus the event.rs for how events are dispatched:

1→/// Footer bar widget — bottom rows of the three-zone TUI layout.
     2→///
     3→/// Renders context-sensitive keybinding hints based on the current [`Mode`].
     4→/// When the terminal height exceeds 30 rows, a secondary F-key bar is shown
     5→/// on a second line (mc-style function key legend).
     6→///
     7→/// Mode-specific legends:
     8→/// - **Normal**: `[h/l]col [j/k]card [↵]actions [Shift+H/L]move [/]filter [n]new [q]quit`
     9→/// - **Detail**: `[j/k]scroll [F3]logs [p]pause [r]retry [Esc]close`
    10→/// - **LogTail**: `[↑↓]scroll [f]follow [c]clear [Esc]close`
    11→/// - **Filter**: `Filter: {query}█  [Esc]clear [↵]confirm`
    12→/// - **ActionPopup**: `[↑↓]select [↵]run [Esc]cancel`
    13→/// - **NewCard**: `New card id: {input}█  [↵]create [Esc]cancel`
    14→/// - **Subshell**: `(subshell active — Ctrl-D to return)`
    15→use ratatui::layout::Rect;
    16→use ratatui::style::{Color, Style};
    17→use ratatui::text::{Line, Span};
    18→use ratatui::widgets::Paragraph;
    19→use ratatui::Frame;
    20→
    21→use crate::ui::app::{App, Mode};
    22→
    23→// ── Constants ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24→
    25→/// Minimum terminal height to show the secondary F-key bar.
    26→const FKEY_BAR_MIN_HEIGHT: u16 = 30;
    27→
    28→/// Block cursor character for input fields.
    29→const BLOCK_CURSOR: &str = "█";
    30→
    31→// ── Legend builders ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32→
    33→/// Build keybinding hint spans for Normal mode.
    34→fn normal_legend() -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
    35→    vec![
    36→        Span::styled("[h/l]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    37→        Span::styled("col ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    38→        Span::styled("[j/k]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    39→        Span::styled("card ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    40→        Span::styled("[↵]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    41→        Span::styled("actions ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    42→        Span::styled("[Shift+H/L]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    43→        Span::styled("move ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    44→        Span::styled("[/]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    45→        Span::styled("filter ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    46→        Span::styled("[n]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    47→        Span::styled("new ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    48→        Span::styled("[q]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    49→        Span::styled("quit", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    50→    ]
    51→}
    52→
    53→/// Build keybinding hint spans for Detail mode.
    54→fn detail_legend() -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
    55→    vec![
    56→        Span::styled("[j/k]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    57→        Span::styled("scroll ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    58→        Span::styled("[F3]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    59→        Span::styled("logs ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    60→        Span::styled("[p]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    61→        Span::styled("pause ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    62→        Span::styled("[r]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    63→        Span::styled("retry ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    64→        Span::styled("[Esc]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    65→        Span::styled("close", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    66→    ]
    67→}
    68→
    69→/// Build keybinding hint spans for LogTail mode.
    70→fn logtail_legend() -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
    71→    vec![
    72→        Span::styled("[↑↓]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    73→        Span::styled("scroll ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    74→        Span::styled("[f]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    75→        Span::styled("follow ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    76→        Span::styled("[c]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    77→        Span::styled("clear ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    78→        Span::styled("[Esc]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    79→        Span::styled("close", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    80→    ]
    81→}
    82→
    83→/// Build keybinding hint spans for Filter mode with the current query.
    84→fn filter_legend(query: &str) -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
    85→    vec![
    86→        Span::styled("Filter: ", Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
    87→        Span::styled(
    88→            format!("{}{}", query, BLOCK_CURSOR),
    89→            Style::default().fg(Color::White),
    90→        ),
    91→        Span::styled("  ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    92→        Span::styled("[Esc]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    93→        Span::styled("clear ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    94→        Span::styled("[↵]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    95→        Span::styled("confirm", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    96→    ]
    97→}
    98→
    99→/// Build keybinding hint spans for ActionPopup mode.
   100→fn action_popup_legend() -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
   101→    vec![
   102→        Span::styled("[↑↓]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
   103→        Span::styled("select ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   104→        Span::styled("[↵]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
   105→        Span::styled("run ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   106→        Span::styled("[Esc]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
   107→        Span::styled("cancel", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   108→    ]
   109→}
   110→
   111→/// Build keybinding hint spans for NewCard mode with the current input.
   112→fn new_card_legend(input: &str) -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
   113→    vec![
   114→        Span::styled("New card id: ", Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
   115→        Span::styled(
   116→            format!("{}{}", input, BLOCK_CURSOR),
   117→            Style::default().fg(Color::White),
   118→        ),
   119→        Span::styled("  ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   120→        Span::styled("[↵]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
   121→        Span::styled("create ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   122→        Span::styled("[Esc]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
   123→        Span::styled("cancel", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   124→    ]
   125→}
   126→
   127→/// Build keybinding hint spans for Subshell mode.
   128→fn subshell_legend() -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
   129→    vec![Span::styled(
   130→        "(subshell active — Ctrl-D to return)",
   131→        Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
   132→    )]
   133→}
   134→
   135→/// Build the secondary F-key bar (mc-style function key legend).
   136→fn fkey_bar() -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
   137→    vec![
   138→        Span::styled("F3", Style::default().fg(Color::Black).bg(Color::Cyan)),
   139→        Span::styled("=logs  ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   140→        Span::styled("F4", Style::default().fg(Color::Black).bg(Color::Cyan)),
   141→        Span::styled("=inspect  ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   142→        Span::styled("F5", Style::default().fg(Color::Black).bg(Color::Cyan)),
   143→        Span::styled("=pause  ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   144→        Span::styled("F8", Style::default().fg(Color::Black).bg(Color::Cyan)),
   145→        Span::styled("=kill  ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   146→        Span::styled("F10", Style::default().fg(Color::Black).bg(Color::Cyan)),
   147→        Span::styled("=quit", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   148→    ]
   149→}
   150→
   151→// ── Main render ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   152→
   153→/// Render the footer bar into the given area.
   154→///
   155→/// Builds context-sensitive keybinding hints based on the current mode.
   156→/// When the terminal height (passed via `terminal_height`) exceeds
   157→/// [`FKEY_BAR_MIN_HEIGHT`], a secondary F-key bar is rendered on a second
   158→/// line — the caller must allocate 2 rows instead of 1 in that case.
   159→pub fn render_footer(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, app: &App, terminal_height: u16) {
   160→    if area.height == 0 || area.width == 0 {
   161→        return;
   162→    }
   163→
   164→    // Build the primary legend line based on current mode.
   165→    let legend_spans = match app.mode {
   166→        Mode::Normal => normal_legend(),
   167→        Mode::Detail => detail_legend(),

entries.content:
All done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Subtask 4-3: LogTail Overlay — Complete

### Files Created
- **`crates/bop-cli/src/ui/widgets/logtail.rs`** — New LogTail overlay widget with:
  - `stylize_log_line()` — ratatui-native colorization (mirrors `logs.rs` ANSI patterns but uses `Style` instead of escape codes)
  - `render_logtail()` — full-height body overlay with bordered block, follow-mode indicator, scrollable log lines
  - 8 unit tests for line styling

### Files Modified
- **`crates/bop-cli/src/ui/widgets/mod.rs`** — Added `logtail` module + `render_logtail` re-export
- **`crates/bop-cli/src/ui/app.rs`** — Added:
  - 7 new fields: `log_scroll`, `log_follow`, `log_tail_card_id`, `log_stdout_pos`, `log_stderr_pos`, `log_stdout_incomplete`, `log_stderr_incomplete`
  - `enter_log_tail()` — reads initial log content (last 200 lines), records file positions, enables follow mode
  - `exit_log_tail()` — clears tailing state, returns to Normal
  - `poll_log_files()` — incremental log reading on tick (250ms), reusing `logs.rs` pattern
  - `read_log_chunk()` — newline-gated streaming: buffers incomplete lines, handles file truncation
  - 6 unit tests for `read_log_chunk` (complete lines, incomplete buffering, cross-read completion, missing file, truncation, no-new-data)
- **`crates/bop-cli/src/ui/input.rs`** — Wired `handle_logtail()` handler (↑↓/j/k scroll, f follow, c clear, Esc exit); updated ActionPopup's Logs action to call `enter_log_tail()`
- **`crates/bop-cli/src/ui/mod.rs`** — Wired LogTail overlay rendering in the `draw()` function

### Key Design Decisions
- **Tick-based polling** (250ms via existing tick event) rather than a separate background task — simpler lifecycle, matches existing `refresh_provider_meters` pattern
- **Newline-gated streaming** with per-file incomplete buffers prevents partial ANSI sequence flicker
- **Follow mode** auto-scroll calculated in render function (doesn't require terminal height in input handler)
- **File truncation detection** resets position for log rotation support
I'll start by understanding the codebase structure, then implement the filter widget and wire it into the existing code.

1→/// TUI application state and event types.
     2→///
     3→/// Defines the core data model for the kanban TUI: columns, card views,
     4→/// navigation state, and the event enum that unifies keyboard input,
     5→/// filesystem changes, and timer ticks into a single async stream.
     6→use std::collections::VecDeque;
     7→use std::fs;
     8→use std::io::{Read, Seek, SeekFrom};
     9→use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
    10→

    20→
    21→// ── Events ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    22→
    23→/// Unified event type for the TUI event loop.
    24→///
    25→/// Background tasks (fs watcher, log reader, timer) send these through
    26→/// `tokio::sync::mpsc::unbounded_channel`. The UI loop processes them
    27→/// sequentially — never blocking the render thread.
    28→#[derive(Debug)]
    29→pub enum AppEvent {
    30→    /// Keyboard input from crossterm.
    31→    Key(KeyEvent),
    32→    /// Terminal resize (cols, rows).
    33→    Resize(u16, u16),
    34→    /// 250ms heartbeat for clock, sparkline, and animation updates.
    35→    Tick,
    36→    /// Filesystem watcher detected card changes — full refresh payload.
    37→    Cards(Vec<KanbanColumn>),
    38→    /// New complete line from the selected card's log tail.
    39→    LogLine(String),
    40→}
    41→
    42→// ── Mode ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    43→
    44→/// Current interaction mode. Determines which keybindings are active
    45→/// and which overlay (if any) is drawn on top of the kanban body.
    46→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    47→pub enum Mode {
    48→    /// Default: column/card navigation, action keys active.
    49→    Normal,
    50→    /// `/` filter active — keystrokes go to nucleo query input.
    51→    Filter,
    52→    /// Enter key opened the action popup overlay.
    53→    ActionPopup,
    54→    /// `d` opened the card detail overlay.
    55→    Detail,
    56→    /// Log tail overlay — full-height scrollable log view.
    57→    LogTail,
    58→    /// `n` inline card creation prompt.
    59→    NewCard,
    60→    /// `!` subshell — TUI suspended, shell active in card worktree.
    61→    Subshell,
    62→}
    63→
    64→// ── KanbanColumn ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    65→
    66→/// Column state names in display order.
    67→pub const COLUMN_STATES: &[&str] = &["pending", "running", "done", "failed", "merged"];
    68→
    69→/// One vertical column in the kanban board.
    70→///
    71→/// Each column corresponds to a card state directory. The `list_state`
    72→/// tracks the currently selected row within the column for ratatui's
    73→/// `StatefulWidget` rendering.
    74→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    75→pub struct KanbanColumn {
    76→    /// State name (e.g. "pending", "running").
    77→    pub state: String,
    78→    /// Cards currently in this state.
    79→    pub cards: Vec<CardView>,
    80→    /// Ratatui selection state for the list widget.
    81→    pub list_state: ListState,
    82→    /// WIP limit for this column (from config `max_concurrent` for "running").
    83→    pub wip_limit: Option<usize>,
    84→    /// Auto-collapsed when card count is 0.
    85→    pub collapsed: bool,
    86→}
    87→
    88→impl KanbanColumn {
    89→    /// Create a new column for the given state with cards.
    90→    pub(crate) fn new(state: &str, cards: Vec<CardView>, wip_limit: Option<usize>) -> Self {
    91→        let collapsed = cards.is_empty();
    92→        let mut list_state = ListState::default();
    93→        if !cards.is_empty() {
    94→            list_state.select(Some(0));
    95→        }
    96→        Self {
    97→            state: state.to_string(),
    98→            cards,
    99→            list_state,
   100→            wip_limit,
   101→            collapsed,
   102→        }
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    /// Returns true when the column is at or over its WIP limit.
   106→    pub fn is_at_wip_limit(&self) -> bool {
   107→        match self.wip_limit {
   108→            Some(limit) => self.cards.len() >= limit,
   109→            None => false,
   110→        }
   111→    }
   112→
   113→    /// WIP saturation fraction (0.0–1.0). Returns 0.0 when no limit is set.
   114→    pub fn wip_saturation(&self) -> f32 {
   115→        match self.wip_limit {
   116→            Some(limit) if limit > 0 => (self.cards.len() as f32 / limit as f32).min(1.0),
   117→            _ => 0.0,
   118→        }
   119→    }
   120→}
   121→
   122→// ── App ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   123→
   124→/// Top-level TUI application state.
   125→///
   126→/// Holds all columns, focus indices, mode, log buffer, and throughput
   127→/// sparkline data. The render layer reads this immutably; event handlers
   128→/// mutate it.
   129→pub struct App {
   130→    /// Kanban columns in display order (pending → running → done → failed → merged).
   131→    pub columns: Vec<KanbanColumn>,
   132→    /// Index into `columns` for the currently focused column.
   133→    pub col_focus: usize,
   134→    /// Active nucleo filter query (None = no filter).
   135→    pub filter: Option<String>,
   136→    /// Current interaction mode.
   137→    pub mode: Mode,
   138→    /// Circular buffer of recent log lines (last 200) from the selected card.
   139→    pub log_buf: VecDeque<String>,
   140→    /// Throughput sparkline samples (last 8 values, cards/hr).
   141→    pub throughput: VecDeque<u8>,
   142→    /// Root path of the .cards directory.
   143→    pub cards_root: PathBuf,
   144→    /// Provider status meters for the header bar.
   145→    pub provider_meters: Vec<ProviderMeter>,
   146→    /// Recent card completions for the scrolling event ticker.
   147→    pub recent_completions: VecDeque<String>,
   148→    /// Monotonic tick counter for ticker scroll animation.
   149→    pub tick_count: u64,
   150→    /// Previous "done" card count for throughput delta tracking.
   151→    pub prev_done_count: usize,
   152→    /// Scroll offset for the Detail overlay (lines scrolled from top).
   153→    pub detail_scroll: usize,
   154→    /// Ratatui selection state for the ActionPopup list widget.
   155→    pub action_list_state: ListState,
   156→    /// Scroll offset for the LogTail overlay (lines scrolled from top).
   157→    pub log_scroll: usize,
   158→    /// Follow mode for LogTail — auto-scrolls to show latest lines.
   159→    pub log_follow: bool,
   160→    /// Card ID currently being tailed in LogTail mode (None when not tailing).
   161→    pub log_tail_card_id: Option<String>,
   162→    /// File position in `logs/stdout.log` for incremental reading.
   163→    pub(crate) log_stdout_pos: u64,
   164→    /// File position in `logs/stderr.log` for incremental reading.
   165→    pub(crate) log_stderr_pos: u64,
   166→    /// Incomplete line buffer for stdout (newline-gated streaming).
   167→    pub(crate) log_stdout_incomplete: String,
   168→    /// Incomplete line buffer for stderr (newline-gated streaming).
   169→    pub(crate) log_stderr_incomplete: String,
   170→}
   171→
   172→/// Maximum number of log lines retained in the circular buffer.
   173→const LOG_BUF_CAPACITY: usize = 200;
   174→
   175→/// Number of throughput sparkline samples.
   176→const THROUGHPUT_SAMPLES: usize = 8;
   177→
   178→impl App {
   179→    /// Create a new App by scanning the filesystem for cards.
   180→    ///
   181→    /// Reads cards from the `.cards` directory using the same pattern as
   182→    /// `list.rs::collect_card_views`. Reads `max_concurrent` from the
   183→    /// bop-core config for the "running" column's WIP limit.
   184→    pub fn new(cards_root: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<Self> {
   185→        let columns = build_columns(cards_root);
   186→
   187→        // Focus the first non-collapsed column, or 0 if all are collapsed.
   188→        let col_focus = columns
   189→            .iter()
   190→            .position(|c| !c.collapsed)
   191→            .unwrap_or(0);
   192→
   193→        let done_count = columns
   194→            .iter()
   195→            .find(|c| c.state == "done")
   196→            .map(|c| c.cards.len())
   197→            .unwrap_or(0);
   198→
   199→        let mut app = App {
   200→            columns,
   201→            col_focus,
   202→            filter: None,
   203→            mode: Mode::Normal,
   204→            log_buf: VecDeque::with_capacity(LOG_BUF_CAPACITY),
   205→            throughput: VecDeque::from(vec![0u8; THROUGHPUT_SAMPLES]),
   206→            cards_root: cards_root.to_path_buf(),
   207→            provider_meters: Vec::new(),
   208→            recent_completions: VecDeque::with_capacity(TICKER_CAPACITY),
   209→            tick_count: 0,
   210→            prev_done_count: done_count,

   217→            log_stderr_pos: 0,
   218→            log_stdout_incomplete: String::new(),
   219→            log_stderr_incomplete: String::new(),
   220→        };
   221→
   222→        // Seed provider meters from providers.json.
   223→        app.refresh_provider_meters();
   224→
   225→        Ok(app)
   226→    }
   227→
   228→    /// Return the currently focused column, if any.
   229→    pub fn focused_column(&self) -> Option<&KanbanColumn> {

entries.content: Commit successful. Now let me update the subtask status and todo list:

Subtask 5-1 is complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Summary

### New file: `crates/bop-cli/src/ui/widgets/filter.rs`
- **`FilterState`** — holds the nucleo `Matcher` and current query string, reused across keystrokes
- **`FilterMatch`** — result of matching a card, containing `title_indices` (sorted, deduplicated char positions for highlighting)
- **`matches()`** — fuzzy-matches against `"{card_id} {card_title}"` haystack using `nucleo::pattern::Pattern` with `CaseMatching::Smart` + `AtomKind::Fuzzy`, then maps combined-haystack indices back to title-only indices
- 6 unit tests covering: empty query, title match, ID match, no match, sorted indices, case insensitivity

### Modified files:
- **`app.rs`** — Added `filter_state: Option<FilterState>` field, plus `enter_filter_mode()`, `confirm_filter()`, `clear_filter()`, and `apply_filter_collapse()` methods
- **`input.rs`** — Wired `/` key in Normal mode → `enter_filter_mode()`. Added `handle_filter()` handler: `Esc` clears, `Enter` confirms, `Backspace` deletes, `Char(c)` appends. Each keystroke triggers `apply_filter_collapse()`
- **`kanban.rs`** — Added `build_title_spans()` for Yellow+BOLD highlighting of matched characters. `render_column()` accepts optional filter matches to hide non-matching cards. `render_kanban()` pre-computes filter matches when filter is active
- **`widgets/mod.rs`** — Registered `filter` module and re-exported `FilterState`

### Verification:
- `cargo build -p bop` — compiles cleanly (no new warnings)
- `cargo test -p bop` — all 808 tests pass (772 + 10 + 17 + 4 + 5)
Let me read all the relevant files first:

entries.detail:
1→/// TUI application state and event types.
     2→///
     3→/// Defines the core data model for the kanban TUI: columns, card views,
     4→/// navigation state, and the event enum that unifies keyboard input,
     5→/// filesystem changes, and timer ticks into a single async stream.
     6→use std::collections::VecDeque;
     7→use std::fs;
     8→use std::io::{Read, Seek, SeekFrom};
     9→use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
    10→
    11→use chrono::Utc;
    12→use crossterm::event::KeyEvent;
    13→use ratatui::widgets::ListState;
    14→
    15→use crate::paths;
    16→use crate::providers::read_providers;
    17→use crate::render::CardView;
    18→use crate::ui::widgets::action::actions_for_state;
    19→use crate::ui::widgets::filter::FilterState;
    20→use crate::ui::widgets::header::{ProviderMeter, ProviderStatus, TICKER_CAPACITY};
    21→
    22→// ── Events ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    23→
    24→/// Unified event type for the TUI event loop.
    25→///
    26→/// Background tasks (fs watcher, log reader, timer) send these through
    27→/// `tokio::sync::mpsc::unbounded_channel`. The UI loop processes them
    28→/// sequentially — never blocking the render thread.
    29→#[derive(Debug)]
    30→pub enum AppEvent {
    31→    /// Keyboard input from crossterm.
    32→    Key(KeyEvent),
    33→    /// Terminal resize (cols, rows).
    34→    Resize(u16, u16),
    35→    /// 250ms heartbeat for clock, sparkline, and animation updates.
    36→    Tick,
    37→    /// Filesystem watcher detected card changes — full refresh payload.
    38→    Cards(Vec<KanbanColumn>),
    39→    /// New complete line from the selected card's log tail.
    40→    LogLine(String),
    41→}
    42→
    43→// ── Mode ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    44→
    45→/// Current interaction mode. Determines which keybindings are active
    46→/// and which overlay (if any) is drawn on top of the kanban body.
    47→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    48→pub enum Mode {
    49→    /// Default: column/card navigation, action keys active.
    50→    Normal,
    51→    /// `/` filter active — keystrokes go to nucleo query input.
    52→    Filter,
    53→    /// Enter key opened the action popup overlay.
    54→    ActionPopup,
    55→    /// `d` opened the card detail overlay.
    56→    Detail,
    57→    /// Log tail overlay — full-height scrollable log view.
    58→    LogTail,
    59→    /// `n` inline card creation prompt.
    60→    NewCard,
    61→    /// `!` subshell — TUI suspended, shell active in card worktree.
    62→    Subshell,
    63→}
    64→
    65→// ── KanbanColumn ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    66→
    67→/// Column state names in display order.
    68→pub const COLUMN_STATES: &[&str] = &["pending", "running", "done", "failed", "merged"];
    69→
    70→/// One vertical column in the kanban board.
    71→///
    72→/// Each column corresponds to a card state directory. The `list_state`
    73→/// tracks the currently selected row within the column for ratatui's
    74→/// `StatefulWidget` rendering.
    75→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    76→pub struct KanbanColumn {
    77→    /// State name (e.g. "pending", "running").
    78→    pub state: String,
    79→    /// Cards currently in this state.
    80→    pub cards: Vec<CardView>,
    81→    /// Ratatui selection state for the list widget.
    82→    pub list_state: ListState,
    83→    /// WIP limit for this column (from config `max_concurrent` for "running").
    84→    pub wip_limit: Option<usize>,
    85→    /// Auto-collapsed when card count is 0.
    86→    pub collapsed: bool,
    87→}
    88→
    89→impl KanbanColumn {
    90→    /// Create a new column for the given state with cards.
    91→    pub(crate) fn new(state: &str, cards: Vec<CardView>, wip_limit: Option<usize>) -> Self {
    92→        let collapsed = cards.is_empty();
    93→        let mut list_state = ListState::default();
    94→        if !cards.is_empty() {
    95→            list_state.select(Some(0));
    96→        }
    97→        Self {
    98→            state: state.to_string(),
    99→            cards,
   100→            list_state,
   101→            wip_limit,
   102→            collapsed,
   103→        }
   104→    }
   105→
   106→    /// Returns true when the column is at or over its WIP limit.
   107→    pub fn is_at_wip_limit(&self) -> bool {
   108→        match self.wip_limit {
   109→            Some(limit) => self.cards.len() >= limit,
   110→            None => false,
   111→        }
   112→    }
   113→
   114→    /// WIP saturation fraction (0.0–1.0). Returns 0.0 when no limit is set.
   115→    pub fn wip_saturation(&self) -> f32 {
   116→        match self.wip_limit {
   117→            Some(limit) if limit > 0 => (self.cards.len() as f32 / limit as f32).min(1.0),
   118→            _ => 0.0,
   119→        }
   120→    }
   121→}
   122→
   123→// ── App ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   124→
   125→/// Top-level TUI application state.
   126→///
   127→/// Holds all columns, focus indices, mode, log buffer, and throughput
   128→/// sparkline data. The render layer reads this immutably; event handlers
   129→/// mutate it.
   130→pub struct App {
   131→    /// Kanban columns in display order (pending → running → done → failed → merged).
   132→    pub columns: Vec<KanbanColumn>,
   133→    /// Index into `columns` for the currently focused column.
   134→    pub col_focus: usize,
   135→    /// Active nucleo filter query (None = no filter).
   136→    pub filter: Option<String>,
   137→    /// Nucleo filter state — holds the matcher and query for fuzzy filtering.
   138→    /// Present when filter mode is active or a confirmed filter is applied.
   139→    pub filter_state: Option<FilterState>,
   140→    /// Current interaction mode.
   141→    pub mode: Mode,
   142→    /// Circular buffer of recent log lines (last 200) from the selected card.
   143→    pub log_buf: VecDeque<String>,
   144→    /// Throughput sparkline samples (last 8 values, cards/hr).
   145→    pub throughput: VecDeque<u8>,
   146→    /// Root path of the .cards directory.
   147→    pub cards_root: PathBuf,
   148→    /// Provider status meters for the header bar.
   149→    pub provider_meters: Vec<ProviderMeter>,
   150→    /// Recent card completions for the scrolling event ticker.
   151→    pub recent_completions: VecDeque<String>,
   152→    /// Monotonic tick counter for ticker scroll animation.
   153→    pub tick_count: u64,
   154→    /// Previous "done" card count for throughput delta tracking.
   155→    pub prev_done_count: usize,
   156→    /// Scroll offset for the Detail overlay (lines scrolled from top).
   157→    pub detail_scroll: usize,
   158→    /// Ratatui selection state for the ActionPopup list widget.
   159→    pub action_list_state: ListState,
   160→    /// Scroll offset for the LogTail overlay (lines scrolled from top).
   161→    pub log_scroll: usize,
   162→    /// Follow mode for LogTail — auto-scrolls to show latest lines.
   163→    pub log_follow: bool,
   164→    /// Card ID currently being tailed in LogTail mode (None when not tailing).
   165→    pub log_tail_card_id: Option<String>,
   166→    /// File position in `logs/stdout.log` for incremental reading.
   167→    pub(crate) log_stdout_pos: u64,
   168→    /// File position in `logs/stderr.log` for incremental reading.
   169→    pub(crate) log_stderr_pos: u64,
   170→    /// Incomplete line buffer for stdout (newline-gated streaming).
   171→    pub(crate) log_stdout_incomplete: String,
   172→    /// Incomplete line buffer for stderr (newline-gated streaming).
   173→    pub(crate) log_stderr_incomplete: String,
   174→}
   175→
   176→/// Maximum number of log lines retained in the circular buffer.
   177→const LOG_BUF_CAPACITY: usize = 200;
   178→
   179→/// Number of throughput sparkline samples.

   182→impl App {
   183→    /// Create a new App by scanning the filesystem for cards.

   185→    /// Reads cards from the `.cards` directory using the same pattern as

   210→            throughput: VecDeque::from(vec![0u8; THROUGHPUT_SAMPLES]),

   212→            provider_meters: Vec::new(),
   213→            recent_completions: VecDeque::with_capacity(TICKER_CAPACITY),

   221→            log_stdout_pos: 0,
   222→            log_stderr_pos: 0,
   223→            log_stdout_incomplete: String::new(),
   224→            log_stderr_incomplete: String::new(),
   225→        };
   226→
   227→        // Seed provider meters from providers.json.
   228→        app.refresh_provider_meters();
   229→
   230→        Ok(app)

1→/// Footer bar widget — bottom rows of the three-zone TUI layout.
     2→///
     3→/// Renders context-sensitive keybinding hints based on the current [`Mode`].
     4→/// When the terminal height exceeds 30 rows, a secondary F-key bar is shown

     6→///
     7→/// Mode-specific legends:

    10→/// - **LogTail**: `[↑↓]scroll [f]follow [c]clear [Esc]close`
    11→/// - **Filter**: `Filter: {query}█  [Esc]clear [↵]confirm`
    12→/// - **ActionPopup**: `[↑↓]select [↵]run [Esc]cancel`

   136→fn fkey_bar() -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
   137→    vec![

   153→/// Render the footer bar into the given area.

   155→/// Builds context-sensitive keybinding hints based on the current mode.

   158→/// line — the caller must allocate 2 rows instead of 1 in that case.
   159→pub fn render_footer(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, app: &App, terminal_height: u16) {
   160→    if area.height == 0 || area.width == 0 {

   162→    }

   167→        Mode::Detail => detail_legend(),
   168→        Mode::LogTail => logtail_legend(),
   169→        Mode::Filter => {
   170→            let query = app.filter.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
   171→            filter_legend(query)

   173→        Mode::ActionPopup => action_popup_legend(),
   174→        Mode::NewCard => {

   176→            // for now show an empty input prompt.
   177→            new_card_legend("")
   178→        }
   179→        Mode::Subshell => subshell_legend(),
   180→    };

   182→    let line1 = Line::from(legend_spans);

   184→    // If terminal is tall enough and we have room, add the F-key bar.

   190→    };
   191→
   192→    let footer = Paragraph::new(lines);
   193→    frame.render_widget(footer, area);
   194→}

entries.content:
Subtask 5-2 is complete. Here's what was implemented:

### Summary

**Files created:**
- `crates/bop-cli/src/ui/widgets/newcard.rs` — NewCard module with `create_card()` function that shells out to `bop new default <id>` via `std::process::Command`

**Files modified:**
- `crates/bop-cli/src/ui/app.rs` — Added `newcard_input: String` field to `App` struct for tracking the card ID being typed
- `crates/bop-cli/src/ui/input.rs` — Wired `'n'` key in Normal mode to enter `Mode::NewCard`, added `handle_newcard()` handler that accepts printable chars, backspace, enter (creates card + refreshes columns), and escape (cancels)
- `crates/bop-cli/src/ui/widgets/footer.rs` — Updated `Mode::NewCard` footer rendering to use the live `app.newcard_input` buffer instead of an empty string
- `crates/bop-cli/src/ui/widgets/mod.rs` — Registered the `newcard` module and exported `create_card`

**Verification:** Build succeeds, all 131 tests pass, committed as `bf9e899`.

entries.detail:
1→/// TUI application state and event types.
     2→///
     3→/// Defines the core data model for the kanban TUI: columns, card views,
     4→/// navigation state, and the event enum that unifies keyboard input,
     5→/// filesystem changes, and timer ticks into a single async stream.
     6→use std::collections::VecDeque;
     7→use std::fs;
     8→use std::io::{Read, Seek, SeekFrom};
     9→use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
    10→
    11→use chrono::Utc;
    12→use crossterm::event::KeyEvent;
    13→use ratatui::widgets::ListState;
    14→
    15→use crate::paths;
    16→use crate::providers::read_providers;
    17→use crate::render::CardView;
    18→use crate::ui::widgets::action::actions_for_state;
    19→use crate::ui::widgets::filter::FilterState;
    20→use crate::ui::widgets::header::{ProviderMeter, ProviderStatus, TICKER_CAPACITY};
    21→
    22→// ── Events ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    23→
    24→/// Unified event type for the TUI event loop.
    25→///
    26→/// Background tasks (fs watcher, log reader, timer) send these through
    27→/// `tokio::sync::mpsc::unbounded_channel`. The UI loop processes them
    28→/// sequentially — never blocking the render thread.
    29→#[derive(Debug)]
    30→pub enum AppEvent {
    31→    /// Keyboard input from crossterm.
    32→    Key(KeyEvent),
    33→    /// Terminal resize (cols, rows).
    34→    Resize(u16, u16),
    35→    /// 250ms heartbeat for clock, sparkline, and animation updates.
    36→    Tick,
    37→    /// Filesystem watcher detected card changes — full refresh payload.
    38→    Cards(Vec<KanbanColumn>),
    39→    /// New complete line from the selected card's log tail.
    40→    LogLine(String),
    41→}
    42→
    43→// ── Mode ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    44→
    45→/// Current interaction mode. Determines which keybindings are active
    46→/// and which overlay (if any) is drawn on top of the kanban body.
    47→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    48→pub enum Mode {
    49→    /// Default: column/card navigation, action keys active.
    50→    Normal,
    51→    /// `/` filter active — keystrokes go to nucleo query input.
    52→    Filter,
    53→    /// Enter key opened the action popup overlay.
    54→    ActionPopup,
    55→    /// `d` opened the card detail overlay.
    56→    Detail,
    57→    /// Log tail overlay — full-height scrollable log view.
    58→    LogTail,
    59→    /// `n` inline card creation prompt.
    60→    NewCard,
    61→    /// `!` subshell — TUI suspended, shell active in card worktree.
    62→    Subshell,
    63→}
    64→
    65→// ── KanbanColumn ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    66→
    67→/// Column state names in display order.
    68→pub const COLUMN_STATES: &[&str] = &["pending", "running", "done", "failed", "merged"];
    69→
    70→/// One vertical column in the kanban board.
    71→///
    72→/// Each column corresponds to a card state directory. The `list_state`
    73→/// tracks the currently selected row within the column for ratatui's
    74→/// `StatefulWidget` rendering.
    75→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    76→pub struct KanbanColumn {
    77→    /// State name (e.g. "pending", "running").
    78→    pub state: String,
    79→    /// Cards currently in this state.
    80→    pub cards: Vec<CardView>,
    81→    /// Ratatui selection state for the list widget.
    82→    pub list_state: ListState,
    83→    /// WIP limit for this column (from config `max_concurrent` for "running").
    84→    pub wip_limit: Option<usize>,
    85→    /// Auto-collapsed when card count is 0.
    86→    pub collapsed: bool,
    87→}
    88→
    89→impl KanbanColumn {
    90→    /// Create a new column for the given state with cards.
    91→    pub(crate) fn new(state: &str, cards: Vec<CardView>, wip_limit: Option<usize>) -> Self {
    92→        let collapsed = cards.is_empty();
    93→        let mut list_state = ListState::default();
    94→        if !cards.is_empty() {
    95→            list_state.select(Some(0));
    96→        }
    97→        Self {
    98→            state: state.to_string(),
    99→            cards,
   100→            list_state,
   101→            wip_limit,
   102→            collapsed,
   103→        }
   104→    }
   105→
   106→    /// Returns true when the column is at or over its WIP limit.
   107→    pub fn is_at_wip_limit(&self) -> bool {
   108→        match self.wip_limit {
   109→            Some(limit) => self.cards.len() >= limit,
   110→            None => false,
   111→        }
   112→    }
   113→
   114→    /// WIP saturation fraction (0.0–1.0). Returns 0.0 when no limit is set.
   115→    pub fn wip_saturation(&self) -> f32 {
   116→        match self.wip_limit {
   117→            Some(limit) if limit > 0 => (self.cards.len() as f32 / limit as f32).min(1.0),
   118→            _ => 0.0,
   119→        }
   120→    }
   121→}
   122→
   123→// ── App ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   124→
   125→/// Top-level TUI application state.
   126→///
   127→/// Holds all columns, focus indices, mode, log buffer, and throughput
   128→/// sparkline data. The render layer reads this immutably; event handlers
   129→/// mutate it.
   130→pub struct App {
   131→    /// Kanban columns in display order (pending → running → done → failed → merged).
   132→    pub columns: Vec<KanbanColumn>,
   133→    /// Index into `columns` for the currently focused column.
   134→    pub col_focus: usize,
   135→    /// Active nucleo filter query (None = no filter).
   136→    pub filter: Option<String>,
   137→    /// Nucleo filter state — holds the matcher and query for fuzzy filtering.
   138→    /// Present when filter mode is active or a confirmed filter is applied.
   139→    pub filter_state: Option<FilterState>,
   140→    /// Current interaction mode.
   141→    pub mode: Mode,
   142→    /// Circular buffer of recent log lines (last 200) from the selected card.
   143→    pub log_buf: VecDeque<String>,
   144→    /// Throughput sparkline samples (last 8 values, cards/hr).
   145→    pub throughput: VecDeque<u8>,
   146→    /// Root path of the .cards directory.
   147→    pub cards_root: PathBuf,
   148→    /// Provider status meters for the header bar.
   149→    pub provider_meters: Vec<ProviderMeter>,
   150→    /// Recent card completions for the scrolling event ticker.
   151→    pub recent_completions: VecDeque<String>,
   152→    /// Monotonic tick counter for ticker scroll animation.
   153→    pub tick_count: u64,
   154→    /// Previous "done" card count for throughput delta tracking.
   155→    pub prev_done_count: usize,
   156→    /// Scroll offset for the Detail overlay (lines scrolled from top).
   157→    pub detail_scroll: usize,
   158→    /// Ratatui selection state for the ActionPopup list widget.
   159→    pub action_list_state: ListState,
   160→    /// Scroll offset for the LogTail overlay (lines scrolled from top).
   161→    pub log_scroll: usize,
   162→    /// Follow mode for LogTail — auto-scrolls to show latest lines.
   163→    pub log_follow: bool,
   164→    /// Card ID currently being tailed in LogTail mode (None when not tailing).
   165→    pub log_tail_card_id: Option<String>,
   166→    /// File position in `logs/stdout.log` for incremental reading.
   167→    pub(crate) log_stdout_pos: u64,
   168→    /// File position in `logs/stderr.log` for incremental reading.
   169→    pub(crate) log_stderr_pos: u64,
   170→    /// Incomplete line buffer for stdout (newline-gated streaming).
   171→    pub(crate) log_stdout_incomplete: String,
   172→    /// Incomplete line buffer for stderr (newline-gated streaming).
   173→    pub(crate) log_stderr_incomplete: String,
   174→    /// Input buffer for NewCard mode — holds the card ID being typed.
   175→    pub newcard_input: String,
   176→}
   177→
   178→/// Maximum number of log lines retained in the circular buffer.
   179→const LOG_BUF_CAPACITY: usize = 200;
   180→
   181→/// Number of throughput sparkline samples.
   182→const THROUGHPUT_SAMPLES: usize = 8;
   183→
   184→impl App {
   185→    /// Create a new App by scanning the filesystem for cards.
   186→    ///
   187→    /// Reads cards from the `.cards` directory using the same pattern as
   188→    /// `list.rs::collect_card_views`. Reads `max_concurrent` from the
   189→    /// bop-core config for the "running" column's WIP limit.
   190→    pub fn new(cards_root: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<Self> {
   191→        let columns = build_columns(cards_root);
   192→
   193→        // Focus the first non-collapsed column, or 0 if all are collapsed.
   194→        let col_focus = columns
   195→            .iter()
   196→            .position(|c| !c.collapsed)
   197→            .unwrap_or(0);
   198→
   199→        let done_count = columns
   200→            .iter()
   201→            .find(|c| c.state == "done")
   202→            .map(|c| c.cards.len())
   203→            .unwrap_or(0);
   204→
   205→        let mut app = App {
   206→            columns,
   207→            col_focus,
   208→            filter: None,
   209→            filter_state: None,
   210→            mode: Mode::Normal,
   211→            log_buf: VecDeque::with_capacity(LOG_BUF_CAPACITY),
   212→            throughput: VecDeque::from(vec![0u8; THROUGHPUT_SAMPLES]),
   213→            cards_root: cards_root.to_path_buf(),
   214→            provider_meters: Vec::new(),
   215→            recent_completions: VecDeque::with_capacity(TICKER_CAPACITY),
   216→            tick_count: 0,
   217→            prev_done_count: done_count,
   218→            detail_scroll: 0,
   219→            action_list_state: ListState::default(),
   220→            log_scroll: 0,
   221→            log_follow: false,
   222→            log_tail_card_id: None,
   223→            log_stdout_pos: 0,
   224→            log_stderr_pos: 0,
   225→            log_stdout_incomplete: String::new(),
   226→            log_stderr_incomplete: String::new(),
   227→            newcard_input: String::new(),
   228→        };
   229→
   230→        // Seed provide

1→/// Footer bar widget — bottom rows of the three-zone TUI layout.
     2→///
     3→/// Renders context-sensitive keybinding hints based on the current [`Mode`].
     4→/// When the terminal height exceeds 30 rows, a secondary F-key bar is shown
     5→/// on a second line (mc-style function key legend).
     6→///
     7→/// Mode-specific legends:
     8→/// - **Normal**: `[h/l]col [j/k]card [↵]actions [Shift+H/L]move [/]filter [n]new [q]quit`
     9→/// - **Detail**: `[j/k]scroll [F3]logs [p]pause [r]retry [Esc]close`
    10→/// - **LogTail**: `[↑↓]scroll [f]follow [c]clear [Esc]close`
    11→/// - **Filter**: `Filter: {query}█  [Esc]clear [↵]confirm`
    12→/// - **ActionPopup**: `[↑↓]select [↵]run [Esc]cancel`
    13→/// - **NewCard**: `New card id: {input}█  [↵]create [Esc]cancel`
    14→/// - **Subshell**: `(subshell active — Ctrl-D to return)`
    15→use ratatui::layout::Rect;
    16→use ratatui::style::{Color, Style};
    17→use ratatui::text::{Line, Span};
    18→use ratatui::widgets::Paragraph;
    19→use ratatui::Frame;
    20→
    21→use crate::ui::app::{App, Mode};
    22→
    23→// ── Constants ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24→
    25→/// Minimum terminal height to show the secondary F-key bar.
    26→const FKEY_BAR_MIN_HEIGHT: u16 = 30;
    27→
    28→/// Block cursor character for input fields.
    29→const BLOCK_CURSOR: &str = "█";
    30→
    31→// ── Legend builders ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32→
    33→/// Build keybinding hint spans for Normal mode.
    34→fn normal_legend() -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
    35→    vec![
    36→        Span::styled("[h/l]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    37→        Span::styled("col ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    38→        Span::styled("[j/k]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    39→        Span::styled("card ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    40→        Span::styled("[↵]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    41→        Span::styled("actions ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    42→        Span::styled("[Shift+H/L]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    43→        Span::styled("move ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    44→        Span::styled("[/]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    45→        Span::styled("filter ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    46→        Span::styled("[n]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    47→        Span::styled("new ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    48→        Span::styled("[q]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    49→        Span::styled("quit", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    50→    ]
    51→}
    52→
    53→/// Build keybinding hint spans for Detail mode.
    54→fn detail_legend() -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
    55→    vec![
    56→        Span::styled("[j/k]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    57→        Span::styled("scroll ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    58→        Span::styled("[F3]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
    59→        Span::styled("logs ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
    60→        Span::styled("[p]", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),

   127→/// Build keybinding hint spans for Subshell mode.
   128→fn subshell_legend() -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
   129→    vec![Span::styled(
   130→        "(subshell active — Ctrl-D to return)",
   131→        Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
   132→    )]
   133→}
   134→
   135→/// Build the secondary F-key bar (mc-style function key legend).
   136→fn fkey_bar() -> Vec<Span<'static>> {
   137→    vec![
   138→        Span::styled("F3", Style::default().fg(Color::Black).bg(Color::Cyan)),
   139→        Span::styled("=logs  ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   140→        Span::styled("F4", Style::default().fg(Color::Black).bg(Color::Cyan)),
   141→        Span::styled("=inspect  ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   142→        Span::styled("F5", Style::default().fg(Color::Black).bg(Color::Cyan)),
   143→        Span::styled("=pause  ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   144→        Span::styled("F8", Style::default().fg(Color::Black).bg(Color::Cyan)),
   145→        Span::styled("=kill  ", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   146→        Span::styled("F10", Style::default().fg(Color::Black).bg(Color::Cyan)),
   147→        Span::styled("=quit", Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
   148→    ]
   149→}
   150→
   151→// ── Main render ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   152→
   153→/// Render the footer bar into the given area.
   154→///
   155→/// Builds context-sensitive keybinding hints based on the current mode.
   156→/// When the terminal height (passed via `terminal_height`) exceeds
   157→/// [`FKEY_BAR_MIN_HEIGHT`], a secondary F-key bar is rendered on a second
   158→/// line — the caller must allocate 2 rows instead of 1 in that case.
   159→pub fn render_footer(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, app: &App, terminal_height: u16) {
   160→    if area.height == 0 || area.width == 0 {
   161→        return;
   162→    }
   163→
   164→    // Build the primary legend line based on current mode.
   165→    let legend_spans = match app.mode {
   166→        Mode::Normal => normal_legend(),
   167→        Mode::Detail => detail_legend(),
   168→        Mode::LogTail => logtail_legend(),
   169→        Mode::Filter => {
   170→            let query = app.filter.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
   171→            filter_legend(query)
   172→        }
   173→        Mode::ActionPopup => action_popup_legend(),
   174→        Mode::NewCard => {
   175→            new_card_legend(&app.newcard_input)
   176→        }
   177→        Mode::Subshell => subshell_legend(),
   178→    };
   179→
   180→    let line1 = Line::from(legend_spans);
   181→
   182→    // If terminal is tall enough and we have room, add the F-key bar.
   183→    let lines = if area.height >= 2 && terminal_height > FKEY_BAR_MIN_HEIGHT {
   184→        let line2 = Line::from(fkey_bar());
   185→        vec![line1, line2]
   186→    } else {
   187→        vec![line1]
   188→    };
   189→
   190→    let footer = Paragraph::new(lines);
   191→    frame.render_widget(footer, area);
   192→}
   193→
   194→#[cfg(test)]
   195→mod tests {
   196→    use super::*;
   197→
   198→    // ── legend builders ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   199→
   200→    #[test]
   201→    fn normal_legend_contains_all_keys() {
   202→        let spans = normal_legend();
   203→        let text: String = spans.iter().map(|s| s.content.as_ref()).collect();
   204→        assert!(text.contains("[h/l]"));
   205→        assert!(text.contains("[j/k]"));
   206→        assert!(text.contains("[↵]"));
   207→        assert!(text.contains("[Shift+H/L]"));
   208→        assert!(text.contains("[/]"));
   209→        as
